halfLife.conditions: plasma elimination half-life in adults 7 ± 1 h (FDA label), independent of dose and route; renally eliminated (~66% unchanged in urine), minimally metabolised. Point value 7 h, band = the ±1 h SD reported.

vd.conditions: the FDA label does not print a numeric volume; it states the volume of distribution is 'close to the volume of intracellular and extracellular water' (i.e. total body water). Total body water is ~0.6 L/kg, the value used here (band 0.5–0.7 L/kg spans the customary reported range). Levetiracetam is <10% protein-bound, so an apparent-volume/true-volume distinction does not arise. Scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time.

F.conditions: oral bioavailability of levetiracetam tablets is 100% (FDA label); tablets and oral solution are bioequivalent. Food does not change the extent of absorption (only lowers Cmax ~20% and delays Tmax). Because F = 1 is measured (not assumed), Vd is a true volume and the oral curve is C = D/V.

tmax.conditions: peak plasma concentrations occur 'in about an hour' after oral dosing in fasted subjects (FDA label). Representative point value 1.0 h.

fda_label.title: KEPPRA (levetiracetam) tablets/oral solution — FDA Structured Product Label, Clinical Pharmacology / Pharmacokinetics (half-life 7 ± 1 h; oral bioavailability 100%; Tmax ~1 h; <10% protein bound; volume of distribution close to total body water; linear kinetics over 500–5000 mg)

notes:
A textbook-clean LINEAR one-compartment drug, curated entirely from the FDA Keppra label. Reported PK: half-life 7 ± 1 h, oral bioavailability 100%, Tmax ~1 h, <10% plasma protein binding, and kinetics that are explicitly 'linear and time-invariant, with low intra- and inter-subject variability' over the 500–5000 mg dose range. This is why levetiracetam is a good teaching compound: superposition is on firm ground (linearity stated by the label, not assumed), and F = 1 makes the IV and oral curves differ only by the absorption phase — the cleanest illustration of oral-vs-IV equivalence in the seed set.

VOLUME: the label characterises the volume of distribution qualitatively ('close to the volume of intracellular and extracellular water') rather than numerically. Total body water is ~0.6 L/kg, which is the value used (band 0.5–0.7 L/kg). Because the drug is <10% protein-bound and F = 1, the volume is genuinely a true distribution volume, not an apparent V/F.

ROUTES: oral (F = 1) and IV are both real (Keppra is marketed as tablets, oral solution, and IV injection; the IV form is given as a ~15-minute infusion). iv_infusion and iv_bolus are both offered with F = 1 by definition; the bolus is illustrative (the product is infused, not pushed).

RENAL ELIMINATION: ~66% is excreted unchanged in urine, so clearance scales with renal function — an educational point about renal drugs, but NOT modelled as a patient-specific adjustment (the bright line: no patient renal input, no dose output). Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
